2606.02276.title: Cross-modal linkage risk in clinical vision-language models
2606.02276.paper_summary: Vision-language models (VLMs) trained on paired chest radiographs and radiology reports learn a shared embedding space that can preserve instance-level image-report correspondence. This poses a privacy risk in settings where radiographs and reports are deliberately kept separate after acquisition, such as image-only data sharing or access-controlled reports, because a de-identified image may be re-linked to its original narrative report through cosine similarity alone. We formalized this as image-to-report retrieval and used public paired cohorts, in which the true pairing is known by design, as ground-truth benchmarks to audit the risk rather than as the privacy scenario. Evaluating VLMs of increasing clinical specialization on 406,241 paired examples from 126,804 patients across MIMIC-CXR (43,793 held-out pairs) and external CheXpert Plus (29,296 pairs), we found that re-linkage rose systematically with specialization: the strongest VLM retrieved the correct report at 15 times chance at a candidate pool of N = 100, 50 times chance at N = 10,000, and well above chance at full-database scale. The signal persisted under pathology-matched hard negatives that removed disease-label shortcuts, indicating correspondence beyond broad diagnostic categories. To reduce it without retraining, we froze both encoders and applied differentially private optimization only to the projection heads defining the alignment layer (epsilon = 0.34, delta = 6x10-6). This reduced Recall@1 by 61.8% at N = 10,000 on MIMIC-CXR and transferred to CheXpert Plus without retraining, while image-side utility was largely preserved: macro AUROC for linear-probe classification across 14 labels shifted only from 79.63% to 79.43%. Targeted DP finetuning of the shared alignment layer can substantially reduce cross-modal re-linkage without materially degrading the image representations that make these models clinically useful.
2606.02276.paper_summary_zh: 視覺-語言模型（VLMs）在配對的胸部X光片和放射學報告上訓練，學習一個共享的嵌入空間，能夠保留實例級別的圖像-報告對應關係。這在放射片和報告在獲取後故意保持分開的環境中構成隱私風險，例如僅圖像數據共享或受控訪問的報告，因為僅透過餘弦相似度，去識別的圖像可能會重新連結到其原始敘述報告。我們將這一現象形式化為圖像到報告的檢索，並使用公共配對隊列，其中真實配對是設計上已知的，作為風險審核的基準，而不是隱私場景。對來自MIMIC-CXR（43,793個保留對）和外部CheXpert Plus（29,296對）的126,804名患者的406,241個配對示例中，評估了隨著臨床專業化增加的VLM，我們發現重新連結隨著專業化系統性上升：最強的VLM在候選池N = 100時以15倍的機會檢索到正確的報告，在N = 10,000時以50倍的機會檢索到，並在全數據庫規模上遠高於隨機機會。該信號在去除了疾病標籤捷徑的病理匹配困難負樣本下依然存在，表明對應關係超越了廣泛的診斷類別。為了在不重新訓練的情況下減少它，我們凍結了兩個編碼器，並僅對定義對齊層的投影頭應用差分隱私優化（epsilon = 0.34，delta = 6x10-6）。這在MIMIC-CXR的N = 10,000下將Recall@1降低了61.8%，並在不重新訓練的情況下轉移到CheXpert Plus，同時圖像端的效用大致得以保留：14個標籤的線性探針分類的宏AUROC僅從79.63%變化到79.43%。針對共享對齊層的差分隱私微調可以大幅減少跨模態重新連結，而不會實質性地降低使這些模型在臨床上有用的圖像表示。
2606.02276.author: Soroosh Tayebi Arasteh et.al.
2606.02276.authors: Soroosh Tayebi Arasteh, Mahshad Lotfinia, Sven Nebelung, Daniel Truhn
